copyright: © 2026 NexVigilant, LLC. PVDSL is a proprietary pharmacovigilance domain-specific language.
title: PVDSL — Pharmacovigilance Domain-Specific Language
description: Compile, evaluate, parse, and introspect PVDSL expressions. PVDSL provides a domain-focused scripting language for signal detection (PRR, ROR, IC, EBGM), causality assessment (Naranjo, WHO-UMC), string similarity (Levenshtein), and mathematical operations. Pure Rust via nexcore-pvdsl.

tools.name: compile
tools.description: Compile a PVDSL expression to bytecode and return the instruction listing. Validates syntax without executing. Useful for pre-flight checks before evaluation.

tools.name: evaluate
tools.description: Evaluate a PVDSL expression with optional variable bindings and return the result. Supports arithmetic, signal detection (signal::prr, signal::ror, signal::ic, signal::ebgm), causality (causality::naranjo), string similarity (meddra::levenshtein), math functions, and chemistry-based capability assessment.

parameters.name: variables
parameters.description: Variable bindings as key-value pairs (values must be numbers, strings, or booleans)

tools.name: functions
tools.description: List all available PVDSL native functions organized by namespace. Namespaces: signal (prr, ror, ic, ebgm, chi_square, fisher, sprt, maxsprt, cusum, mgps), causality (naranjo, who_umc, rucam), meddra (levenshtein, similarity), risk (sar, es, monte_carlo), date (now, diff_days), classify (hartwig_siegel), math (abs, sqrt, pow, log, ln, exp, min, max, floor, ceil, round), chem (arrhenius, michaelis, hill, henderson, halflife, sqi).

tools.name: parse
tools.description: Parse and validate a PVDSL expression, returning the abstract syntax tree (AST). Shows the program structure including statements, expressions, and metadata without executing.
